ops.title: 2026-07-08 token-wastage audit verdicts — cache invalidation is the headline (14%), CI-watch confirmed non-issue, deferred core tools the cleanest fix
ops.body:
Audit of 245 sessions / 3,482M input-tok-eq (2026-06-24→07-08; all cc-pool accounts share one `~/.claude/projects`). Report artifact: https://claude.ai/code/artifact/fb256ae2-6e19-4035-b3d0-06c2c1c035da

**Verdicts (all survived adversarial refutation):**
- **Cache-invalidation rewrites = headline waste: 487M tok-eq (14%)**, sub lane 310M > main 177M. Prefix mutations re-cache the whole conversation at 2×; the durable prefix is only ~16-22K tokens. Purest fixable slice: **ToolSearch loading deferred CORE tools** (ExitPlanMode ×173, Task* ×457, SendMessage, Monitor) — keep those ~11 resident, defer only rare tools. ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH=false globally is NET-NEGATIVE (+28-47M replay).
- Control-returns (background-task notifications, teammate messages, post-idle prompts) price by main-context size — "main holds only final answers" is the remediation.
- **CI-watch after push: confirmed non-issue** (290 calls, ~185 tok each, 0.005% of corpus, no polling loops). Batching nudges, read-guard changes, hook trimming: also non-issues / already shipped.
- Marathon sessions (~10 outliers, 300-500K median context on the 1M model) hold the replay excess (~243M); fix = phase-boundary handoff, not a global compact hook (worst offender already compacted 8×).
- Fork discipline: decompose implementation forks past ~80 turns; sub lane is 72.5% of all spend by design (work relocation), floor per fork only 2-3%.
- TTL rewrites (69M) are 100% human latency at the 1h cache ceiling — only lever is a lean context before idle.

**Why:** remediations were report-only; none implemented as of 2026-07-08.
**How to apply:** before proposing token-efficiency work, check this ranking — tool-output-size interventions are noise (<1%); cache-lane interventions are where the money is. Measurement rules in [[cc-transcript-usage-accounting]].
